response.justification: Aisha Okonkwo demonstrates strong software engineering fundamentals, deep technical versatility across multiple languages and domains (e.g., embedded systems, real-time data, AI-agent infrastructure), and a proven ability to ship production systems. However, her lack of explicit experience with experimentation platforms, A/B testing frameworks, or growth systems (core to the role) raises concerns. Her recent roles emphasize full-stack development and platform engineering, but not the specialized experimentation systems described in the job. She meets most technical requirements but lacks direct alignment with growth/experimentation key responsibilities.
strengths: Strong production-grade engineering experience across multiple languages and systems (e.g., C++, D, JavaScript, Node.js, Python) with demonstrated scalability (e.g., millions of HTTP requests/sec at Dunnhumby).

Proven track record of building foundational systems (e.g., GISCollective, MCP agent infrastructure) and mentoring teams (e.g., Refactoring Dojos, Selenium courses), aligning with leadership expectations.
Hands-on technical depth in telemetry, metrics, and distributed systems (e.g., real-time metadata handling, Kubernetes deployments, low-latency service design).
concerns: No explicit experience with experimentation platforms, A/B testing, or growth systems (e.g., feature flagging, rollout automation, or measurement frameworks) in the resume.

Limited evidence of translating ambiguous business problems into measurable engineering outcomes within growth/experimentation contexts (e.g., no mention of metrics, bias detection, or rollout-risk management).
While she has AI-agent infrastructure experience, there is no clear demonstration of using AI tools to accelerate engineering velocity in growth/experimentation systems.
